summary: End-to-end testing of Petals Atelier v2 light-luxury pivot. Backend 26/26 pytest pass. Frontend light theme (#FAFAF7) confirmed across pages, 6-item nav, portfolio grid+filter+lightbox+CTA, consultation prefill (service + ref_title), Collection→Product→AddToCart→Cart, admin login + dashboard with Orders/Inquiries/Products tabs. All critical flows working.

issues: Event Date field uses browser-native HTML5 date input (mm/dd/yyyy) rather than a shadcn Calendar / Popover component — inconsistent with premium brand styling used elsewhere (luxury aesthetic expects custom date picker).

passed_tests: User register + login + duplicate register rejection
GET /api/portfolio returns 9+ items; filter by category (wedding/sympathy/corporate/house/window); /api/portfolio/{id}; 404 on unknown
GET /api/delivery/options — no Sundays, >= today+2, Saturday £8.99, others £5.99, box/ribbon colors present
POST /api/orders creates order with standard / saturday fees (free when subtotal >= £50)
Frontend: homepage light bg rgb(250,250,247), nav has Shop/Weddings/Sympathy/Corporate/House Installs/Portfolio
Frontend: /portfolio — 9 cards, filter tabs All/Weddings/Sympathy/Corporate/House/Window, Weddings filter narrows to 2, lightbox dialog opens with title/tags/'Enquire a similar piece' CTA
Frontend: /consultation?service=corporate&ref_title=Test%20Ref — service dropdown prefilled 'Corporate / Event', ref_title injected as 'REFERENCE · TEST' and message textarea 'Interest in: Test Ref'
Frontend: /collection shows 8 products, click → /product/:id, Product detail exposes box color / ribbon / message personalization, Add to Basket persists, Cart shows item with Proceed to Checkout
Frontend: Admin login flow → /admin dashboard renders stats (ORDERS 15, PRODUCTS 8, CLIENTS 7, REVENUE £0) + Orders/Inquiries/Products tabs
action_items: (Low priority / optional) Replace native <input type='date'> in ConsultationPage Event Date with shadcn Calendar + Popover to match luxury brand tone.
(Info) Admin Revenue is £0 because all orders are status=pending / payment_status!=paid — this is expected (Stripe test key). Consider seeding one paid order to demonstrate revenue in the dashboard.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py is 1117 lines — approaching the 700-line guideline. Consider splitting into routers (auth, products, portfolio, orders, admin) for maintainability.
OrderCreate.delivery_address is Dict (unstructured) — consider a typed sub-model (line1, city, postcode, country) for validation.
Legacy /api/backend_test.py at /app root is no longer needed since tests moved to /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py.
Legacy /app/frontend/src/pages/SympathyPage.js / WeddingsPage.js were not specifically tested but routes exist — ensure light-theme consistency.
test_credentials: admin@petalsatelier.com / admin123 (confirmed working). TEST_ prefix used for new users.
seed_data_creation: None — relied on existing seed v2-light-luxury. Portfolio contains 9 items, products 8, admin dashboard reports 15 orders / 7 clients.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest suite at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py covers auth, catalog, portfolio, inquiries, delivery, orders (Sunday/too-soon/Saturday/standard-fee), admin. Delivery address is a dict, not a string. Free-delivery kicks in at subtotal >= £50 so many luxury orders get delivery_fee=0. Portfolio categories in seed: wedding/sympathy/corporate/house/window.
